ddls:
CREATE TABLE "jurisdictions" (
  "jurisdiction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each jurisdiction */,
  "jurisdiction_name" TEXT /* Name of the jurisdiction */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the jurisdiction */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the jurisdiction (people per square kilometer) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the jurisdiction */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the jurisdiction */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region of the jurisdiction */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the jurisdiction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("jurisdiction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test record */,
  "jurisdiction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the jurisdiction where the tests were conducted */,
  "tests_last_7_days" INTEGER /* Number of tests conducted in the last 7 days */,
  "tests_last_7_days_per_100k" REAL /* Number of tests conducted in the last 7 days per 100,000 population */,
  "total_tests" INTEGER /* Total number of tests conducted */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the tests were conducted */,
  "test_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test type used */,
  "positive_tests" INTEGER /* Number of positive tests */,
  "negative_tests" INTEGER /* Number of negative tests */,
  "pending_tests" INTEGER /* Number of pending tests */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_jurisdiction_id FOREIGN KEY ("jurisdiction_id") REFERENCES jurisdictions ("jurisdiction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic record */,
  "jurisdiction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the jurisdiction where the demographic data applies */,
  "age_distribution" TEXT /* Age distribution of the jurisdiction (e.g., 0-19, 20-39, etc.) */,
  "gender_distribution" TEXT /* Gender distribution of the jurisdiction (e.g., male, female, etc.) */,
  "ethnicity_distribution" TEXT /* Ethnicity distribution of the jurisdiction (e.g., White, Asian, etc.) */,
  "population_pyramid" TEXT /* Population pyramid of the jurisdiction (e.g., a graphical representation of population distribution) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_jurisdiction_id FOREIGN KEY ("jurisdiction_id") REFERENCES jurisdictions ("jurisdiction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, policymaker, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "query_parameters" TEXT /* Query parameters used for filtering the data (e.g., date range, jurisdiction) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_types" (
  "test_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test type */,
  "test_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the test type (e.g., PCR, rapid antigen) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test type */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the test type (as a percentage) */,
  "processing_time" INTEGER /* Average processing time for the test type (in minutes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "test_result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test the result belongs to */,
  "test_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test type used */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the test (e.g., positive, negative) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the test result was recorded */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient who took the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES tests ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_type_id") REFERENCES test_types ("test_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_positivity_rates" (
  "positivity_rate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each positivity rate record */,
  "jurisdiction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the jurisdiction where the positivity rate applies */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the positivity rate was recorded */,
  "positivity_rate" REAL /* Percentage of tests that were positive */,
  "moving_average" REAL /* Moving average of the positivity rate over the last 7 days */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("positivity_rate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_positivity_rates_jurisdiction_id FOREIGN KEY ("jurisdiction_id") REFERENCES jurisdictions ("jurisdiction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vaccination_data" (
  "vaccination_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vaccination record */,
  "jurisdiction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the jurisdiction where the vaccination data applies */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the vaccination data was recorded */,
  "fully_vaccinated" INTEGER /* Number of people fully vaccinated */,
  "partially_vaccinated" INTEGER /* Number of people partially vaccinated */,
  "vaccine_type" TEXT /* Type of vaccine used (e.g., Pfizer, Moderna) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vaccination_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vaccination_data_jurisdiction_id FOREIGN KEY ("jurisdiction_id") REFERENCES jurisdictions ("jurisdiction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hospitalization_data" (
  "hospitalization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hospitalization record */,
  "jurisdiction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the jurisdiction where the hospitalization data applies */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the hospitalization data was recorded */,
  "hospitalized" INTEGER /* Number of people hospitalized */,
  "icu" INTEGER /* Number of people in ICU */,
  "ventilators" INTEGER /* Number of ventilators in use */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hospitalization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hospitalization_data_jurisdiction_id FOREIGN KEY ("jurisdiction_id") REFERENCES jurisdictions ("jurisdiction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mortality_data" (
  "mortality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mortality record */,
  "jurisdiction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the jurisdiction where the mortality data applies */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the mortality data was recorded */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Number of deaths */,
  "age_distribution" TEXT /* Age distribution of deaths (e.g., 0-19, 20-39, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mortality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mortality_data_jurisdiction_id FOREIGN KEY ("jurisdiction_id") REFERENCES jurisdictions ("jurisdiction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the patient */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the patient */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the patient */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the patient */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the patient */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patient_symptoms" (
  "symptom_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each symptom record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient who reported the symptom */,
  "symptom" TEXT /* Name of the symptom (e.g., fever, cough) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the symptom (e.g., mild, moderate, severe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("symptom_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_symptoms_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patient_exposures" (
  "exposure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exposure record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient who reported the exposure */,
  "exposure" TEXT /* Name of the exposure (e.g., close contact, travel) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the exposure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("exposure_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_exposures_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)
